umm.digiquilt.view
Class Tile

java.lang.Object
  umm.digiquilt.view.Tile

public class Tile
extends Object

Represents the view of a single tile. It can either calculate the proper location to draw the tile, or it can simply paint itself using a Graphics object.

Version:
$Revision: 1.1 $
Author:
Jason Biatek, last changed by $Author: lamberty $ on $Date: 2008/01/22 17:50:24 $

Method Detail

getX

public int getX(int patchSize)
Parameters:
patchSize -
Returns:
the x coordinate that the shape should be placed at, if a Patch is patchSize by patchSize

getY

public int getY(int patchSize)
Parameters:
patchSize -
Returns:
the y coordinate that the shape should be placed at, if a Patch is patchSize by patchSize

getRotation

public double getRotation()
Returns:
the amount of rotation that should be applied to the shape

getColor

public Color getColor()
Returns:
the color that the shape should be filled with

getFabric

public Fabric getFabric()
Returns:
the fabric for this Tile

getIndex

public int getIndex()
Returns:
the index of this Tile.

getTileShape

public static Shape getTileShape(int patchSize)
Create and return a Shape which can be used to draw this Tile. It will not be rotated or translated or anything, but it will be the correct size. (this is so that things like animation, which want to do averages of two different tiles, can get the proper coordinates and then do custom drawing themselves, with whatever rotations they want)

Parameters:
patchSize -
Returns:
the shape

paintTile

public void paintTile(Graphics2D g2,
                      int patchSize)
Paint this tile using the given Graphics object.

Parameters:
g2 -
patchSize -

getTilesFromBlock

public static List<Tile> getTilesFromBlock(Block block)
Parameters:
block -
patchSize -
Returns:
a list containing a Tile for each tile in the block
